questions.question: Where is the origin (0,0) on the GraphicsProgram canvas?
questions.explanation: ACM Graphics uses a screen coordinate system with (0,0) at the top-left; x increases right, y increases downward.

questions.question: Which call properly places a GRect r at coordinates (100, 200)?
questions.explanation: You can place while adding using add(r, x, y). setLocation is fine too, but only after the object is added; option A alone does not add the object.

questions.question: What does pause(16) in an animation loop most directly control?
questions.explanation: pause(16) delays about 16 ms per frame, targeting roughly 60 frames per second.

questions.question: Which method moves a GObject relative to its current position?
questions.explanation: move(dx, dy) offsets the current position by the given deltas.

questions.question: To bounce a ball off the right wall, which condition is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Check the ball’s right edge: x + width. When it reaches or exceeds the canvas width, reverse dx.

questions.question: Which call ensures a rectangle’s interior shows the chosen color?
questions.explanation: You must mark the shape as filled and set its fill color; setColor only changes the outline if not filled.

questions.question: What is the best way to detect an object directly under a point (x, y)?
options: C) contains(x, y) on every object manually
questions.explanation: getElementAt(x, y) queries the canvas and returns the topmost GObject at that location.

questions.question: You wrote an infinite while loop without pause(). What is the most likely symptom?
options: C) The UI becomes unresponsive and animation runs too fast to see
questions.explanation: Without pause, the loop monopolizes CPU and starves event handling and repainting, freezing the UI.

questions.question: Which method changes the layering so an object appears above others?
questions.explanation: sendToFront() places the GObject at the front of the z-order.

questions.question: You want arrow keys to move a player. What must be done first?
options: A) Call addKeyListeners() and override keyPressed(KeyEvent e)
questions.explanation: Register for keyboard events with addKeyListeners() and implement keyPressed/keyReleased to handle input.
